author: AdityaD_Shah
body:
Whatever the choice
Jerome Powell has some very very difficult decisions to make which could alter the entire US economy!

(7/7)

author: AdityaD_Shah
body:
What can the Fed do?
The Fed must not stop hiking interest rates as inflation will then flag off a huge crisis in the economy.

It could temporarily slow down interest rate hikes while the US govt insures deposits and Bank runs settle down.

(6/7)

author: AdityaD_Shah
body:
🏦 Not hike rates at all
Fed could day they want to pause and assess the systemic risks for the small banks

But run the potential risk of inflation staying persistently higher or triggering an acceleration and thus a second wave of inflation.

Negative for the US economy.

(5/7)

author: AdityaD_Shah
body:
25 bps hike the way out?

🏦 Fed could hike 25bps to signal that it is serious about tackling inflation.

At the same time, it could slow down the interest rate hike signaling it understands the systemic problems for small banks

(4/7)

author: AdityaD_Shah
body:
What will the US Fed do now?

🏦 Previously penciled-
A 50bps hike looks out of the picture.

As more and more banks face huge MTM losses on the bond portfolio the Fed cannot hike aggressively anymore
More banks will face an existential crisis,

(3/7)

author: AdityaD_Shah
body:
How can US stop bank run on small banks?

The Government must now step in and do what it did for Silicon Valley Bank

The FDIC must insure $18 trillion worth of deposits in the U.S. banking system

This will help calm down the nerves and stop the deposit run on small banks

(2/7)

author: Rollin_Trades
body: Statement notes that risks to achieving employment and inflation goals “are roughly in balance”

author: Rollin_Trades
body: Statement says committee has gained greater confidence that inflation is moving sustainably toward 2% goal

author: Rollin_Trades
body: Fed tweaks language to note job gains “have slowed;” says inflation “has made further progress toward the committee's 2% objective but remains somewhat elevated”

author: Rollin_Trades
body: Statement says that “in considering additional adjustments” to rates, officials will assess incoming data, evolving outlook and balance of risks

author: Rollin_Trades
body: Statement adds language to say the committee is “strongly committed to supporting maximum employment” in addition to returning inflation to its 2% goal

author: Rollin_Trades
body: Median rate forecast for 2025 falls to 3.4% from 4.1% in June, implying four additional quarter- point moves next year

author: Rollin_Trades
body: “Dot plot” of rate projections shows the median official expected to lower rates by a percentage point by year-end, implying two more quarter-point cuts or one larger, half-point cut; nine of 19 officials penciled in 75 basis points of cuts or less

author: Rollin_Trades
body: Fed Governor Michelle Bowman votes against decision in favor of a smaller, quarter-point cut, the first such dissent since June 2022 and the first time a Fed governor has dissented since September 2005

author: Rollin_Trades
body: Federal Open Market Committee votes 11 to 1 to lower benchmark rate by 50 basis points to target range of 4.75%-5.0%, the first rate cut in more than four years
